# User Interface Overview

This guide highlights the three visual pieces that appear when you open the web app.

## Waterfall Plot

![Waterfall plot](data:image/png;base64,)

The waterfall is a constantly updating picture of the nearby airwaves.  
* **Left to right** shows different radio frequencies.  
* **Top to bottom** shows time, with the newest information at the top.  
* **Color** indicates how strong a signal is at a given moment.

It is similar to taking repeated photos of the spectrum and stacking them. Stronger
signals paint bright streaks while quiet areas remain dark.

## Control Panel

![Control panel](data:image/png;base64,)

The panel offers two ways to configure a scan:

* **Presets** – ready‑made settings for common tasks. Choose one and press **Start**.
* **Advanced controls** – every option exposed for manual tweaking when you need
  fine control over gains, sample rate, or search bands.

Use the **Start** and **Stop** buttons to begin or halt scanning at any time.

Start with a preset to get results quickly and switch to the advanced tab once you
understand which knobs matter for your situation.

## Detected Signals Table

![Detected signals table](data:image/png;base64,)

Each row summarises a transmission that crossed the threshold.

* **Frequency** – the center of the signal in MHz.
* **Modulation** – how the information was encoded (e.g. FSK, PSK, AM).
* **Strength** – a relative measure of how loud the signal was.
* **Start / End** – timestamps showing when the burst was first and last seen.

Clicking a row reveals more detail and decoding options for that signal.
